title: Buttondown vs AWeber (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Buttondown vs AWeber: pricing, features, and which email marketing tool is right for you.
intro: Buttondown and AWeber are both established email marketing platforms, but they cater to different audiences. Buttondown excels for technical users who value markdown editing and developer-friendly features, while AWeber prioritizes simplicity and broader functionality for content creators. This comparison will help you choose the right platform based on your specific needs.
choose_a_if: You're a developer or technical writer comfortable with markdown
You need robust API access and advanced automations
You want the most affordable entry point at $9/month
choose_b_if: You're a beginner wanting an intuitive interface and visual editor
You need landing pages and RSS-to-email functionality
You want a larger free tier (500 vs 100 subscribers)
You're interested in earning affiliate commissions

faq.a: Neither is universally 'better'—it depends on your needs. Buttondown excels for developers and markdown enthusiasts with strong API features, while AWeber offers broader functionality and simplicity for general bloggers and solopreneurs. AWeber's higher G2 (4.2 vs 4.8) and Trustpilot (4.0 vs 4.7) ratings show Buttondown has higher user satisfaction, though both are well-regarded.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or AWeber?
faq.a: Buttondown is cheaper on paid plans at $9/month versus AWeber's $15/month. However, AWeber's free tier allows 500 subscribers compared to Buttondown's 100, making AWeber more cost-effective if you're starting out and want to grow without paying immediately.

faq.q: Can I switch from AWeber to Buttondown?
faq.a: Both platforms support import/export functionality, making migration possible. You can export your subscriber list from AWeber and import it into Buttondown. However, you'll need to manually recreate campaigns and automations, as these typically don't transfer between platforms.
